risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Affecting Project Implementation
risk_summary: The project faces persistent substantial political and governance risks that constrain implementation progress. The document indicates that while key activities are underway, implementation acceleration is needed, suggesting governance-related delays in advancing reforms and institutional capacity building.
evidence_quote: While key activities are underway, project implementation should be accelerated to ensure the sustainability of project achievements.

risk_title: Institutional Capacity and Governance Constraints in Mining Sector Reforms
risk_summary: Multiple critical mining sector regulatory reforms remain incomplete or delayed, including Protected Area Code, Water Code, and mining permits exclusion from classified forests. These delays reflect governance fragility and institutional capacity limitations affecting the project's core objectives.
evidence_quote: Mining permits excluded from classified forests (Yes/No) Comments on achieving targets Classified forests outlined and excluded from licensing possibilities in the mining cadastre A directory of protected areas was produced in 2019, and the revision of the protected areas network began at the end of 2022. However, this data is not currently included in the mining cadastre.

risk_title: Governance Tensions Between Mining and Environmental Protection Mandates
risk_summary: Coordination challenges between the Ministry of Mining and environmental authorities are evident in the delayed integration of protected areas data into the mining cadastre. A dialogue with the Ministry of Mining is only scheduled for the second half of 2025, indicating governance friction.
evidence_quote: With the support of AT OGPRNRF, work to identify overlaps has been underway since November 2024. As part of the protected area review process, a dialogue with the Ministry of Mining is scheduled for second half of 2025.

risk_title: Weak Institutional Capacity for Environmental and Social Monitoring
risk_summary: The project's environmental and social monitoring capacity remains underdeveloped, with the modernization of the environmental and social assessment database still not achieved as of June 2025. This reflects institutional fragility in governance structures.
evidence_quote: b. Modernization and generalization of the database of the environmental and social assessment database to all relevant projects (Yes/No) Comments on achieving targets Capacity of AGEE in identifying and mapping all ongoing or planned infrastructure projects (dams, roads, railways, mines, etc.) to be environmentally and socially monitored June 2025: The TA for AGEE started with implementation support.
